title: Maze Product Outcomes, Repository Baseline, Accessibility, and Evidence · Runtime Lens: Player Outcome
summary: A team says “build a maze solver” without defining the player task, puzzle rules, assistance, supported input, repository state, or proof of success. Predict the model, event, graph, solver, accessibility, failure, and evidence before reading the governing source. Planned artifact: Add and verify the player outcome increment in player, repository, accessibility, and evidence charter. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence def…
objectives: Define player, task, puzzle rules, success, failure, session length, and observable value before selecting algorithms.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-maze-solver-python cumulative accessible desktop maze portfolio.

Browser execution uses deterministic pure-Python grid, graph, search, state, and scheduler models
with original fixed in-memory fixtures only. Native Tcl, Tk, tkinter, windows, Canvas, fonts,
focus, input devices, assistive technology, files, platform timing, installers, display scaling,
packaging, and production effects require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class MazePolicy:
    rows: int = 12
    columns: int = 16
    seed: int = 314159
    maximum_cells: int = 16_384
    reduced_motion: bool = False


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class MazeEvidence:
    revision: str
    topology_digest: str
    action_trace_digest: str
    accessibility_review_id: str
    artifact_digest: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Python maze solver portfolio ready"

content.code:
# Worked focus: maze-player-outcome — Define player, task, puzzle rules, success, failure, session length, and observable value before selecting algorithms.
# Evidence: maze-worked-maze-outcomes-repo-accessibility-2
# Competency: maze-worked-maze-outcomes-repo-accessibility-2.
# Case 7417: a keyboard-only puzzle tournament.
# Operating constraint: the prior good state must survive failure.
# Changed case: change the grid dimensions and preserve every invariant.
def worked_maze_outcomes_repo_accessibility_2(player_task: str = "compare-solvers", acceptance_cases: int = 8, repository_clean: bool = True, accessibility_plan: bool = True, native_limits_named: bool = True) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_worked2 = "worked2-2dekrow-7417"
    assert evidence_variant_worked2
    evidence_axes_worked2 = (
        "gridworked2",
        "topology7417",
        "search2dekrow",
        "eventworked27417",
        "access7417worked2",
        "recovery2dekrow7417",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_worked2)) == 6
    if not player_task.strip() or acceptance_cases < 4:
        return False, acceptance_cases, "undefined-outcome"
    if not repository_clean:
        return False, acceptance_cases, "repository-baseline"
    if not accessibility_plan:
        return False, acceptance_cases, "accessibility-gap"
    if not native_limits_named:
        return False, acceptance_cases, "native-overclaim"
    assert acceptance_cases >= 4
    return True, acceptance_cases, "bounded-maze-charter"

Keep its defaults runnable, assert an invariant, and return observable changed-case evidence. Browser code must not import or claim native tkinter, Tcl, Tk, windows, Canvas, fonts, focus, input devices, assistive technology, files, platform timing, installers, or packaged release behavior; verify those boundaries later with Python 3.14.6, recorded Tcl and Tk patch levels, native widget and display tests, keyboard and assistive-technology playtests, representative profiles, clean platform-native PyInstaller 6.21 builds, migration, rollback, recovery, and release gates.

content.code:
# Evidence: maze-player-outcome-vqyri1
# Competency: maze-player-outcome.
# Case 8357: a classroom algorithm comparison.
# Operating constraint: resource use must remain below an explicit budget.
# Changed case: inject one invalid wall, stale callback, or corrupted field and reject it.
def evidence_maze_player_outcome_vqyri1(player_task: str = "compare-solvers", acceptance_cases: int = 8, repository_clean: bool = True, accessibility_plan: bool = True, native_limits_named: bool = True) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_vqyri1 = "vqyri1-1iryqv-8357"
    assert evidence_variant_vqyri1
    evidence_axes_vqyri1 = (
        "gridvqyri1",
        "topology8357",
        "search1iryqv",
        "eventvqyri18357",
        "access8357vqyri1",
        "recovery1iryqv8357",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_vqyri1)) == 6
    if not player_task.strip() or acceptance_cases < 4:
        return False, acceptance_cases, "undefined-outcome"
    if not repository_clean:
        return False, acceptance_cases, "repository-baseline"
    if not accessibility_plan:
        return False, acceptance_cases, "accessibility-gap"
    if not native_limits_named:
        return False, acceptance_cases, "native-overclaim"
    assert acceptance_cases >= 4
    return True, acceptance_cases, "bounded-maze-charter"

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Define player, task, puzzle rules, success, failure, session length, and observable value before selecting algorithms.

hints: For Maze solving, Tkinter, and Python Defend the Player Outcome model · Theory Player Outcome, name the player, revision, Python, Tcl and Tk versions, grid, coordinate, seed, topology, frontier, path, event, state, render, accessibility, package, recovery, and native-transfer boundary, Player Outcome precondition, or invariant that must hold.
